```{r}
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    calcium_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex,grade))

calcium_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band        = "Analysis",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Calcium",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

calcium_final_results <- calcium_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "calcium"
  )
```
\newpage

## Urate {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Most ranges lie between ~2-5mmol/24h. There is high heterogeneity, and meta-regression modestly narrows the range, suggesting a central tendency. Age seems to slightly modify spread but not location of the range. There appear to be differences between sexes, although these are not significant and the analyses appear to be underpowered. Race analyses are underpowered with mostly single studies and wide ranges. No differences on Wald tests. On meta-regression of the overall dataset, the model did not account for any heterogeneity (R2 = 0%), and the only significant difference was between the sexes (for women: -0.77 mmol/24h, p=0.024). The RCT subanalyses have results within the respective ranges of the other analyses. The wider spread of the other analyses suggests they are more representative of the wider population. <br>
	We therefore suggest using **sex-stratified main results**. It should be noted that there were substantially fewer male participants, and this result should be interpreted with caution.
<br>

### Plot
```{r}
male_plot <- urate_ma_2nd_analysis$male$plot %+%
  subset(urate_ma_2nd_analysis$male$plot$data, age_band == "Overall") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1,5.5))

female_plot <- urate_ma_2nd_analysis$female$plot %+%
  subset(urate_ma_2nd_analysis$female$plot$data, age_band == "Overall") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1,5.5))

urate_final_plot <- (male_plot / female_plot) + plot_layout(axes = "collect")

urate_final_plot
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
urate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>% 
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group != "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Urate Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
urate_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(urate_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type, male_female) %>%
  filter(male_female != "mixed") %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(male_female)
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  group_by(sex_group) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Urate"
  )

urate_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Urate Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
urate_male_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # RCTs + Observational cohorts
  risk_of_bias = "Serious", # See bias analysis
  inconsistency = "Not Serious", # similar ranges throughout analyses
  indirectness = "Not Serious", # Large cohorts 
  imprecision = "Not Serious", # Large cohorts with precise metrics 
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", # Large I2 / Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE, # Effect seems similar throughout sub-analyses
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE # No meaningful adjustment with MR 
)

urate_female_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # RCTs + Observational cohorts
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", # See bias analysis
  inconsistency = "Serious", # Wider variation in values than in men
  indirectness = "Not Serious", # Large cohorts
  imprecision = "Serious", # Wider variation in values than in men 
  publication_bias = "Strongly Suspected", # Large I2 / Cochran's Q
  large_effect = FALSE, # Effect varies throughout sub-analyses
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE # No meaningful adjustment with MR 
)

urate_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Male", "Female"),
  grade = c(urate_male_grade$grade_rating,
            urate_female_grade$grade_rating)
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

urate_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(urate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    age_band = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    urate_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

urate_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for urate",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

urate_final_results <- urate_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "urate"
  )
```
\newpage

## Creatinine {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
There is substantial heterogeneity in all analyses. There are clear and significant differences between sexes and age-bands. However, age stratification dramatically reduces the number of eligible studies and reduces the number of participants, reducing the power of these analyses. Race sub-analyses are underpowered with mostly single studies and wide ranges. Meta-regression suggests that women have significantly lower values than men. <br>
	Could potentially use **sex stratified ranges** only as observed adult population distributions.
<br>

### Plot
```{r}
creatinine_final_plot <- (creat_ma_2nd_analysis$male$plot %+% 
  subset(creat_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall"))) / (creat_ma_2nd_analysis$female$plot %+% 
  subset(creat_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall"))) + plot_layout(axes = "collect")

creatinine_final_plot
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
creat_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>% 
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group != "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Creatinine Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
creatinine_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(creatinine_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type, male_female) %>%
  filter(male_female != "mixed") %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(male_female)
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  group_by(sex_group) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Creatinine"
  )

creatinine_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Creatinine Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
creatinine_male_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", 
  risk_of_bias = "Serious", # Most studies at serious risk of bias
  inconsistency = "Serious",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Serious",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", # Significant Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

creatinine_female_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed",
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", # Low ROB analysis comparable to overall female result
  inconsistency = "Serious",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Serious",
  publication_bias = "Suspected", # Significant Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

creatinine_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Male", "Female"),
  grade = c(creatinine_male_grade$grade_rating,
            creatinine_female_grade$grade_rating)
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

creatinine_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(creat_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    age_band = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    creatinine_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

creatinine_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for creatinine",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

creatinine_final_results <- creatinine_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "creatinine"
  )
```
\newpage

## Oxalate {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
There is significant variation across age, sex and race analyses along with high heterogeneity in most categories, save for some subgroups. These subgroups tend to be limited to small datasets with a small number of studies. The main analysis meta-regression demonstrates a significantly lower result as the proportion of women increases (estimate = -0.15, 95% CI: -0.22-0.07, p<0.001). Likewise increasing age seems to significantly lower the result, although this decrease reduces as age bands increase. Sample size has no significant effect. As R2=30% we suggest using the **meta-regression results** as the expected range - this is consistent with the low risk of bias subanalysis and RCT only subanalysis. 
<br>

### Plot
```{r}
oxalate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(oxalate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression"))
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
oxalate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Meta-Regression")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Oxalate Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
oxalate_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(oxalate_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Oxalate"
  )

oxalate_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Oxalate Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
oxalate_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "RCT", # Overall result consistent with RCT subanalysis
  risk_of_bias = "Not Serious", # Overall result consistent with Low ROB subanalysis
  inconsistency = "Not Serious", # Consistent across subanalyses
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Not Serious", # Consistent across subanalyses
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", # Significant Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

oxalate_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = oxalate_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

oxalate_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(oxalate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, meta_regression = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    oxalate_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

oxalate_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Oxalate",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

oxalate_final_results <- oxalate_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "oxalate"
  )
```
\newpage

## pH {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Although there is substantial heterogeneity across most analyses, the absolute spread of values is relatively tight. There is little absolute difference between sex, age and race with no statistical difference between them. The race sub-analysis suffers from few studies and is likely underpowered. Meta-regression shows stability in the overall result.
	As results seem to be consistent across sensitivity and sub-analyses, we recommend that a single pooled adult range is used.
<br>

### Plot
```{r}
ph_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(ph_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall"))
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
ph_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>% 
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "ph Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
ph_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(ph_sd) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine ph"
  )

ph_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine pH Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
ph_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "RCT", # Correlation with RCTs
  risk_of_bias = "Not Serious", # Very similar results between main result + low ROB subanalysis
  inconsistency = "Low", # Strongly consistent throughout studies
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant cochran's Q + I2 high
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

ph_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = ph_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

ph_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(ph_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    ph_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

ph_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for pH",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

ph_final_results <- ph_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "pH"
  )
```
\newpage

## Citrate {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Substantial heterogeneity across all analyses. There seem to be differences between sexes, but these are not significant on Wald test. Age and race subanalyses are underpowered. There seems to be significant instability in the lower limit result and therefore these results should be interpreted with caution.	<br>
	On Risk of bias sub-analysis, the low risk of bias studies tend to have a tighter interval than the main overall meta-analysis, and are more consistent with the published literature. We therefore suggest using the Low risk of bias overall sub-analysis as a conservative estimate of the expected range.
<br>

### Plot
```{r}
citrate_final_plot <- citrate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+%
  subset(citrate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, age_band == "Overall" & quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,5.5))

citrate_final_plot
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
citrate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>% 
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>% filter(
    quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Citrate Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
citrate_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(citrate_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Citrate"
  )

citrate_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Citrate Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
citrate_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # Mixed overall Result
  risk_of_bias = "Not Serious", #Low RoB sub-analysis used for estimation
  inconsistency = "Serious", # Marked variation in values
  indirectness = "Moderate", 
  imprecision = "Not Serious", # Large number of patients in each analysis
  publication_bias = "Suspected", # Significant Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

citrate_grade <- citrate_grade %>%
  as_tibble() %>% 
  mutate(
    sex = c("Overall"),
    grade = grade_rating,
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)) 

citrate_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(citrate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, meta_regression = FALSE, low_rob = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    citrate_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex,grade, certainty_score))

citrate_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Citrate",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

citrate_final_results <- citrate_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "citrate"
  )
```
\newpage

## Phosphate {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Substantial heterogeneity across all analyses, with most subanalyses limited to a small number of studies. There seem to be differences between sexes, but these are not significant on Wald test. Age and race subanalyses are underpowered, with race sub-analyses often limited to single studies or no data at all. Upper limit appears quite unstable across analyses. Minimal differences on Wald tests, but significant amounts of missing data limiting analyses. <br>
	We recommend using the Overall analysis for the expected range. This has the most number of studies, and is a conservative estimate (especially given it remains within the bounds of existing expected ranges), despite significant heterogeneity (Cochran's Q p<0.005) <br>

### Plot
```{r}
phosphate_final_plot <- phosphate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+%
  subset(phosphate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data,
         quality_filter == "All Studies" & age_band == "Overall") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 40))

phosphate_final_plot
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
phosphate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  filter(quality_filter == "All Studies") %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Phosphate Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
phosphate_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(phosphate_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    ) 
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Phosphate"
  )

phosphate_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Phosphate Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
phosphate_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "RCT", # Overall result similar to RCT
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", # Studies with serious risk of bias included
  inconsistency = "Serious", # marked variation between sensitivity analyses
  indirectness = "Moderate", 
  imprecision = "Serious", # marked variation between sensitivity analyses suggests imprecision
  publication_bias = "Suspected", # Cochran's Q significant
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
) 

phosphate_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = phosphate_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

phosphate_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(phosphate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    age_band = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    phosphate_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

phosphate_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for phosphate",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

phosphate_final_results <- phosphate_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "phosphate"
  )
```
\newpage

## Sodium {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
There is substantial variability across all analyses. There appear to be differences in sex and age bands, although only age-band 30-40 demonstrates significant differences between sexes (all other wald tests are non-significant). There are reductions in heterogeneity in specific scenarios e.g. males aged 40-50. However, this is likely due to these analyses being underpowered. Sex specific ranges are at high risk of bias. <br>
  We suggest using the Overall, Low risk of bias subanalysis as a conservative estimate of the true expected range. The main overall result differs markedly with inclusion of high risk of bias studies, and the meta-regression pulls the result more in the direction of the low risk of bias analysis. <br>


### Plot
```{r}
sodium_final_plot <- sodium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+%
  subset(sodium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, age_band == "Overall" & quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(50,270))

sodium_final_plot
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
sodium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    age_band %in% c("Overall")
  ) %>% filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis"
  ) %>% filter(
    quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Sodium Meta-analysis Results by Age Band",
    subtitle = "Random-effects meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
sodium_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(sodium_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Sodium"
  )

sodium_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Sodium Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
sodium_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # Mix of Observational + RCTs
  risk_of_bias = "Not Serious", # Low ROB subanalysis used
  inconsistency = "Serious", # Inconsistency across analyses
  indirectness = "Moderate", 
  imprecision = "Not Serious", # Good level of precision
  publication_bias = "Suspected", # Significant cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

sodium_grade <- sodium_grade %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    sex = "Overall",
    grade = grade_rating,
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

sodium_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(sodium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, low_rob = TRUE, meta_regression = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    sodium_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, certainty_score, grade))

sodium_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Sodium",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

sodium_final_results <- sodium_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "sodium"
  )
```
\newpage


## Potassium {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
High heterogeneity throughout analyses, with substantial numbers of missing strata. The strata with data seem to be mainly underpowered in the race/age-stratified sub-analyses. There were no significant differences on Wald test. Meta-regression slightly reduces the intervals and I2, but substantial heterogeneity remains suggesting study-level covariates only partially explaining variability. <br>
	We recommend the overall meta-regression may be used as a pooled population distribution/expected distribution across heterogenous studies. <br>


### Plot
```{r}
potassium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(potassium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression"))
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
potassium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Potassium Meta-analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
potassium_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(potassium_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine potassium"
  )

potassium_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Potassium Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
potassium_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", 
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", # Low + Low/Moderate ROB subanalyses aren't dissimilar to MR result
  inconsistency = "Moderate",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", # Significant Cochran's Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

potassium_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = potassium_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

potassium_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(potassium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, meta_regression = TRUE, low_rob = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    potassium_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

potassium_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Potassium",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

potassium_final_results <- potassium_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "potassium"
  )
```
\newpage

## Magnesium {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
High heterogeneity throughout analyses, with substantial, albeit non-significant, variation between strata. No clear consistent fluctuation between age groups. Meta-regression slightly reduces the intervals and I2, but substantial heterogeneity remains suggesting study-level covariates only partially explaining variability. <br>
  Meta-analysis of RCTs demonstrates similar heterogeneity, however the results are broadly in line with the remainder of the analyses. Risk of bias subanalyses are consistent with the RCT only and meta-regression result. <br>
	We recommend using the meta-regression results as a conservative estimate of the true population expected range. <br>


### Plot
```{r}
magnesium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(magnesium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression"))
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
magnesium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>% 
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    ),
    sex_group = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Magnesium Meta-analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
magnesium_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(magnesium_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Magnesium"
  )

magnesium_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine Magnesium Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
magnesium_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "RCT", #RCTs have similar ranges to MR result
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", #consistent results + replicated in Low RoB subanalyses
  inconsistency = "Moderate",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

magnesium_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = magnesium_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

magnesium_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(magnesium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, meta_regression = TRUE, low_rob = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 1), " – ", round(upper_95, 1)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    magnesium_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

magnesium_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Magnesium",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

magnesium_final_results <- magnesium_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "magnesium"
  )
```
\newpage

## Ammonium {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Heterogeneity remains high across analyses, however there is reasonable consistency across strata. Meta-regression reduces both interval and I2, suggesting at least partial adjustment for between study variation. Few studies in men alone and no meta-regression in this group, and therefore the 30-40 vs 60+ significant wald test result is not interpretable. Race sub-analyses are underpowered. <br>
  There is the suggestion of age-related decline, however these analyses are underpowered and therefore not robust enough to utilise. <br>
	We recommend the overall low risk of bias subanalysis as a conservative estimate of the whole population expected range. <br>

### Plot
```{r}
ammonium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(ammonium_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall") & quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only")
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
ammonium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Overall"),
    quality_filter == "Low Risk of Bias Only"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = "Overall"
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Ammonium Meta-Analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
ammonium_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(ammonium_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Ammonium"
  )

ammonium_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine ammonium Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
ammonium_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # RCTs + Cohorts
  risk_of_bias = "Low", #Low RoB result being used
  inconsistency = "Moderate",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

ammonium_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = ammonium_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

ammonium_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(ammonium_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE, low_rob = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    ammonium_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

ammonium_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Ammonium",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

ammonium_final_results <- ammonium_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "ammonium"
  )
```
\newpage

## Chloride {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
Substantial heterogeneity throughout analyses. Minimal data for age and race stratifications. Meta-regression does reduce interval and I2 in both male and female analyses. <br>
	We recommend using the overall result given the poor amount of evidence in other strata, and its consistency with the risk of bias subanalyses. However, this is a conservative estimate.
 <br>


### Plot
```{r}
chloride_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(chloride_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall") & quality_filter == "All Studies")
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
chloride_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Overall"),
    quality_filter == "All Studies"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = "Overall"
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Chloride Meta-analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
chloride_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(chloride_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine chloride"
  )

chloride_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine chloride Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
chloride_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # Mixture of RCTs and Cohorts
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", #consistent with low RoB analyses
  inconsistency = "Moderate",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

chloride_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = chloride_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

chloride_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(chloride_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    chloride_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

chloride_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Chloride",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

chloride_final_results <- chloride_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "chloride"
  )
```
\newpage

## Sulphate {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
There are few studies in this analysis, with wide variation in results and high heterogeneity throughout the different strata. The meta-regression result is not valid. There is marked variation between men and women, although non-significant and appear due to variation within the substantially larger female cohort.<br>
	The overall meta-analysis result has the most robust evidence given the similarity to RCT results. Low RoB sub-analyses are underpowered but fit within the overall meta-analysis result. THerefore this should be used as a conservative estimate. <br>


### Plot
```{r}
sulphate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(sulphate_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Overall") & quality_filter == "All Studies")
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
sulphate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Overall"),
    quality_filter == "All Studies"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = str_to_title(sex_group)
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Sulphate Meta-analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Analysis"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
sulphate_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(sulphate_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Sulphate"
  )

sulphate_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine sulphate Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
sulphate_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "RCT", #Consistent with RCT results
  risk_of_bias = "Moderate", # Overall result consistent with RoB sub-analyses 
  inconsistency = "Strongly Suspected", # Power limited
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

sulphate_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = sulphate_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

sulphate_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(sulphate_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    sulphate_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

sulphate_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Sex",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Sulphate",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

sulphate_final_results <- sulphate_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "sulphate"
  )
```
\newpage

## Urea {.tabset .tabset-pills}
### Comment
There were minimal data for urea. Given there were minimal differences between sexes, and improvement in I2 with meta-regression, we suggest utilising the overall meta-regression result as the representative pooled population distribution. <br>


### Plot
```{r}
urea_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot %+% 
  subset(urea_ma_2nd_analysis$main_meta_analysis$plot$data, 
         age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression"))
```
\newpage

### Results Table
```{r}
urea_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results %>%
  filter(
    sex_group == "main meta-analysis",
    age_band %in% ("Meta-Regression")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    I2              = round((I2 * 100), 0),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ as.character(round(Q_pval, 2))
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    ),
    sex_group = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex_group,
    total_participants,
    pooled_estimate,
    pooled_sd,
    ci_95,
    I2,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    sex_group          = "Analysis",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    pooled_estimate = "Pooled Estimate",
    pooled_sd       = "SD",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    I2              = html("I<sup>2</sup> (%)"),
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    columns = c(total_participants, pooled_estimate, pooled_sd, ci_95, I2, Q, Q_pval)
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Urea Meta-analysis Results",
    subtitle = "Meta-Regression"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*I² quantifies heterogeneity; Q p-value tests heterogeneity across studies.*"
    )
  )
```
\newpage

### Bias analysis
```{r}
urea_rob <- urine_ma2 %>%
  drop_na(urea_sd_mmol_24h) %>%
  left_join(
    overall_bias, by = "study_name"
  ) %>%
  select(study_name, overall, study_type) %>%
  mutate(
    overall = case_when(
      is.na(overall) ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ overall
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(study_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(overall) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    study_name = "Study",
    overall    = "Risk of Bias",
    study_type = "Study Type"
  ) %>%
  data_color(
    columns = overall,
    fn = function(x) {
      case_when(
        x == "Low"      ~ "#90EE90",  
        x == "Moderate" ~ "#FFD700",  
        x == "High"     ~ "#FF6B6B",  
        TRUE            ~ "#FFFFFF"
      )
    }
  ) %>%
  opt_all_caps() %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    overall
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Risk of Bias by Study and Age Band for 24 Hour Urine Urea"
  )

urea_rob
```
\newpage

### 24 Hour Urine urea Suggested Expected Range inc. GRADE
```{r}
urea_grade <- calculate_grade(
  study_design= "Mixed", # RCT result different
  risk_of_bias = "Very Serious", #Minimal studies with lower RoB
  inconsistency = "Serious",
  indirectness = "Moderate",
  imprecision = "Moderate",
  publication_bias = "Strongly suspected", #significant Q
  large_effect = TRUE,
  dose_response = FALSE,
  confounders_reduce_effect = FALSE
)

urea_grade <- cbind(
  sex = c("Overall"),
  grade = urea_grade$grade_rating
) %>% as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    grade_rating = grade_symbol(grade)
  )

urea_final_results <- get_final_ma_results(urea_ma_2nd_analysis$ma_results, sex_specific = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pooled_estimate = round(pooled_estimate, 2),
    pooled_sd       = round(pooled_sd, 2),
    Q               = round(Q, 2),
    Q_pval = case_when(
      Q_pval < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
      Q_pval < 0.01  ~ "<0.01",
      Q_pval < 0.05  ~ "<0.05",
      TRUE           ~ sprintf("%.2f", Q_pval)
    ),
    ci_95 = paste0(
      round(lower_95, 0), " – ", round(upper_95, 0)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    age_band,
    total_participants,
    ci_95,
    Q,
    Q_pval
  ) %>%
  cbind(
    urea_grade) %>%
  mutate(grade_rating = paste0(grade, " ", grade_rating)) %>%
  select(-c(sex, grade))

urea_final_results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Meta-Regression",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised Meta-Analysis Results for Urea",
    subtitle = "Subdivided by Age Band"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  )

urea_final_results <- urea_final_results %>% 
  mutate(
    urine_component = "urea"
  )
```
\newpage

# Final Table of All Results {.tabset .tabset-pills}
## All MA Results
```{r}
final_table <- rbind(volume_final_results,
                     calcium_final_results,
                     urate_final_results,
                     creatinine_final_results,
                     oxalate_final_results,
                     ph_final_results,
                     citrate_final_results,
                     phosphate_final_results,
                     sodium_final_results,
                     potassium_final_results,
                     magnesium_final_results,
                     ammonium_final_results,
                     chloride_final_results,
                     sulphate_final_results,
                     urea_final_results
                     )

component_cols <- c(
  "Ammonium"   = "#E2ECE9",
  "Calcium"    = "#FDE2E4",
  "Chloride"   = "#E0F2FE",
  "Citrate"    = "#FFF1C1",
  "Creatinine" = "#E2F0CB",
  "Magnesium"  = "#F1C0E8",
  "Oxalate"    = "#CDE7F0",
  "Ph"         = "#F8F9FA",
  "Phosphate"  = "#FFE5EC",
  "Potassium"  = "#D0F4DE",
  "Sodium"     = "#D3F8E2",
  "Sulphate"   = "#E4C1F9",
  "Urate"      = "#FFD6A5",
  "Urea"       = "#FAD2E1",
  "Volume"     = "#EADCF8"
)

final_gt <- final_table %>%
  mutate(urine_component = case_when(
    urine_component == "ph" ~ "pH",
    urine_component == "creat" ~ "Creatinine",
    TRUE ~ str_to_title(urine_component)
  )) %>%
  group_by(urine_component) %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_label(
    age_band       = "Meta-Analysis Type",
    total_participants  = "Patients, n",
    ci_95           = "95% CI",
    Q               = "Q statistic",
    Q_pval          = "Q p-value",
    grade_rating    = "GRADE Rating"
  ) %>%
  cols_align(
    align = "center",
    -age_band
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Finalised 24 Hour Urine Expected Range Suggestions",
    subtitle = "Recommendations based on meta-analyses with most robust evidence"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = md(
      "*Q p-value = Cochran's Q heterogeneity test; RoB = Risk of Bias*"
    )
  ) %>%
  opt_row_striping() %>%
  tab_options(
    row.striping.background_color = "lightgrey"  
  )

row_groups <- final_gt[["_row_groups"]]

for (comp in names(component_cols)) {

  matching <- row_groups[str_detect(row_groups, comp)]

  final_gt <- final_gt %>%
    tab_style(
      style = list(
        cell_fill(color = component_cols[comp]),
        cell_text(weight = "bold")
      ),
      locations = cells_row_groups(groups = matching)
    )
}

final_gt
```
\newpage

## Comparison to Current Expected Range
```{r}
summary_table_separated <- summary_table_data %>%
  mutate(
    sex_group = case_when(
      str_detect(urine_component, regex("women only", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Women",
      str_detect(urine_component, regex("men only", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Men",
      str_detect(urine_component, regex("overall", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Overall",
      TRUE ~ "Overall"
    ),
    urine_component = urine_component %>%
      str_remove(regex("\\s*-\\s*(men only|women only|overall)", ignore_case = TRUE)) %>%
      str_trim()
  ) %>% 
  arrange(urine_component)

proposed_lookup <- final_table %>%
  mutate(
    urine_component = str_to_title(urine_component),
    sex_group = case_when(age_band == "Male" ~ "Men",
                          age_band == "Female" ~ "Women",
                          age_band == "Main Meta-Analysis" ~ "Overall",
                          age_band == "Meta-Regression" ~ "Overall",
                          TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    `Expected Range` = ci_95,
    "Expected Range GRADE Rating" = grade_rating,
    join_key = str_to_lower(urine_component),
    .keep = "unused"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(urine_component,
                sex_group,
                `Expected Range`,
                `Expected Range GRADE Rating`,
                join_key) %>%
  mutate(
    sex_group = case_when(
      is.na(sex_group) ~ "Overall",
      
      TRUE ~ sex_group
    )
  )

gt_tbl <- summary_table_separated %>%
  mutate(join_key = str_to_lower(str_remove(urine_component, "\\s*\\(.*\\)"))) %>%
  left_join(
    proposed_lookup %>% select(join_key, sex_group, `Expected Range`, `Expected Range GRADE Rating`),
    by = c("join_key", "sex_group")
  ) %>%
  select(-join_key) %>%
  bind_rows(
    proposed_lookup %>%
      filter(!join_key %in% c("ph")) %>% 
      anti_join(
        summary_table_separated %>%
          mutate(join_key = str_to_lower(str_remove(urine_component, "\\s*\\(.*\\)"))),
        by = c("join_key", "sex_group")
      ) %>%
      mutate(urine_component = case_when(
        join_key == "volume" ~ "Volume (L / 24hrs)",
        join_key == "ph" ~ "Ph",
        TRUE ~ paste0(str_to_title(join_key), " (Mmol / 24hrs)")
      )) %>%
      select(-join_key)
  ) %>%
  arrange(factor(sex_group, levels = c("Overall", "Men", "Women")), urine_component) %>%
  group_by(urine_component) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "urine_component") %>%
  cols_move_to_start(columns = sex_group) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = list(cell_fill(color = "#FFF3CD"), cell_text(weight = "bold")),
    locations = list(
      cells_body(columns = `Expected Range`),
      cells_column_labels(columns = `Expected Range`)
    )
  ) %>%
  fmt_missing(columns = everything(), missing_text = "-") %>%
  tab_style(style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
            locations = cells_body(columns = sex_group)) %>%
  cols_label(sex_group = "Sex", urine_component = "Urine Component") %>%
  cols_align("center",
             c(EAU, NDH, NHS, Litholink, `Mayo Clinic`,`Expected Range`, `Expected Range GRADE Rating`)) %>%
  tab_style(style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),
            locations = cells_column_labels(everything())) %>%
  opt_row_striping() %>%
  tab_options(table.font.size = 12,
              row.striping.background_color = "lightgrey")

# Apply row group colours
row_groups <- gt_tbl[["_row_groups"]]
for (comp in names(component_cols)) {
  matching <- row_groups[str_detect(row_groups, comp)]
  if (length(matching) > 0) {
    gt_tbl <- gt_tbl %>%
      tab_style(
        style = list(cell_fill(color = component_cols[comp]), cell_text(weight = "bold")),
        locations = cells_row_groups(groups = matching)
      )
  }
}

gt_tbl
```
\newpage
